001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.collections;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.CacheMode.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.SystemUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
023
024import java.util.*;
025import java.util.concurrent.*;
026import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
027
028import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
029
030/**
031 * Simple in-memory cache for storing and retrieving objects by two-part keys.
032 *
033 * <h5 class='section'>Overview:</h5>
034 * <p>
035 * This class uses {@link java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap} internally to provide a thread-safe caching layer with automatic
036 * value computation, cache eviction, and statistics tracking for two-part composite keys. It's designed for
037 * caching expensive-to-compute or frequently-accessed objects indexed by two keys.
038 *
039 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
040 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
041 *    <li>Thread-safe concurrent access without external synchronization
042 *    <li>Two-part composite key support
043 *    <li>Automatic cache eviction when maximum size is reached
044 *    <li>Lazy computation via {@link Function2} supplier pattern
045 *    <li>Default supplier support for simplified access
046 *    <li>Built-in hit/miss statistics tracking
047 *    <li>Optional logging of cache statistics on JVM shutdown
048 *    <li>Can be disabled entirely via builder or system property
049 * </ul>
050 *
051 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
052 * <p class='bjava'>
053 *    <jc>// Create a cache with default supplier</jc>
054 *    Cache2&lt;ClassLoader,Class&lt;?&gt;,ClassMeta&gt; <jv>metaCache</jv> = Cache2
055 *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(ClassLoader.<jk>class</jk>, Class.<jk>class</jk>, ClassMeta.<jk>class</jk>)
056 *       .maxSize(500)
057 *       .supplier((cl, c) -&gt; createClassMeta(cl, c))
058 *       .build();
059 *
060 *    <jc>// Retrieve using default supplier</jc>
061 *    ClassMeta <jv>meta1</jv> = <jv>metaCache</jv>.get(<jv>classLoader</jv>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
062 *
063 *    <jc>// Or override the supplier</jc>
064 *    ClassMeta <jv>meta2</jv> = <jv>metaCache</jv>.get(<jv>classLoader</jv>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>, () -&gt; customMeta);
065 * </p>
066 *
067 * <h5 class='section'>Cache Behavior:</h5>
068 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
069 *    <li>When a key pair is requested:
070 *       <ul>
071 *          <li>If the key exists in the cache, the cached value is returned (cache hit)
072 *          <li>If the key doesn't exist, the supplier is invoked to compute the value
073 *          <li>The computed value is stored in the cache and returned (cache miss)
074 *       </ul>
075 *    <li>When the cache exceeds {@link Builder#maxSize(int)}, the entire cache is cleared
076 *    <li>If the cache is disabled, the supplier is always invoked without caching
077 *    <li>Null keys always bypass the cache and invoke the supplier
078 * </ul>
079 *
080 * <h5 class='section'>Environment Variables:</h5>
081 * <p>
082 * The following system properties can be used to configure default cache behavior:
083 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
084 *    <li><c>juneau.cache.mode</c> - Cache mode: NONE/WEAK/FULL (default: FULL, case-insensitive)
085 *    <li><c>juneau.cache.maxSize</c> - Maximum cache size before eviction (default: 1000)
086 *    <li><c>juneau.cache.logOnExit</c> - Log cache statistics on shutdown (default: <jk>false</jk>)
087 * </ul>
088 *
089 * <h5 class='section'>Thread Safety:</h5>
090 * <p>
091 * This class is thread-safe and can be safely used from multiple threads without external synchronization.
092 * However, note that when the cache is cleared due to exceeding max size, there's a small window where
093 * multiple threads might compute the same value. This is acceptable for most use cases as it only affects
094 * performance, not correctness.
095 *
096 * <h5 class='section'>Performance Considerations:</h5>
097 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
098 *    <li>Cache operations are O(1) average time complexity
099 *    <li>The {@link #get(Object, Object, java.util.function.Supplier)} method uses
100 *       {@link java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap#putIfAbsent(Object, Object)}
101 *       to minimize redundant computation in concurrent scenarios
102 *    <li>When max size is exceeded, the entire cache is cleared in a single operation
103 *    <li>Statistics tracking uses {@link AtomicInteger} for thread-safe counting without locking
104 * </ul>
105 *
106 * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
107 * <p class='bjava'>
108 *    <jc>// Simple cache with defaults</jc>
109 *    Cache2&lt;String,String,Config&gt; <jv>cache</jv> = Cache2.<jsm>of</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, Config.<jk>class</jk>).build();
110 *
111 *    <jc>// Cache with custom configuration</jc>
112 *    Cache2&lt;String,Integer,User&gt; <jv>userCache</jv> = Cache2
113 *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>, User.<jk>class</jk>)
114 *       .maxSize(500)
115 *       .logOnExit()
116 *       .supplier((tenant, id) -&gt; userService.findUser(tenant, id))
117 *       .build();
118 *
119 *    <jc>// Disabled cache for testing</jc>
120 *    Cache2&lt;String,String,Object&gt; <jv>disabledCache</jv> = Cache2
121 *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, Object.<jk>class</jk>)
122 *       .disableCaching()
123 *       .build();
124 * </p>
125 *
126 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5>
127 * <ul>
128 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Cache}
129 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="../../../../../index.html#juneau-commons">Overview &gt; juneau-commons</a>
130 * </ul>
131 *
132 * @param <K1> The first key type. Can be an array type for content-based key matching.
133 * @param <K2> The second key type. Can be an array type for content-based key matching.
134 * @param <V> The value type.
135 */
136@SuppressWarnings({
137   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
138})
139public class Cache2<K1,K2,V> {
140
141   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
142   private static final String ARG_supplier = "supplier";
143
144   /**
145    * Builder for creating configured {@link Cache2} instances.
146    *
147    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
148    * <p class='bjava'>
149    *    Cache2&lt;String,Integer,User&gt; <jv>cache</jv> = Cache2
150    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>, User.<jk>class</jk>)
151    *       .maxSize(200)
152    *       .logOnExit()
153    *       .build();
154    * </p>
155    *
156    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5>
157    * <ul>
158    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Cache2#of(Class, Class, Class)}
159    * </ul>
160    *
161    * @param <K1> The first key type.
162    * @param <K2> The second key type.
163    * @param <V> The value type.
164    */
165   public static class Builder<K1,K2,V> {
166
167      // Resolved once per JVM.  Looking these up per builder is expensive because Settings iterates every
168      // registered PropertySource (including the manifest scanner), and Cache builders are created very frequently.
169      private static final CacheMode DEFAULT_CACHE_MODE = env("juneau.cache.mode", CacheMode.FULL);
170      private static final int DEFAULT_MAX_SIZE = env("juneau.cache.maxSize", 1000);
171      private static final boolean DEFAULT_LOG_ON_EXIT = env("juneau.cache.logOnExit", false);
172
173      CacheMode cacheMode;
174      int maxSize;
175      String id;
176      boolean logOnExit;
177      boolean threadLocal;
178      Function2<K1,K2,V> supplier;
179
180      Builder() {
181         cacheMode = DEFAULT_CACHE_MODE;
182         maxSize = DEFAULT_MAX_SIZE;
183         logOnExit = DEFAULT_LOG_ON_EXIT;
184         id = "Cache2";
185      }
186
187      /**
188       * Builds a new {@link Cache2} instance with the configured settings.
189       *
190       * @return A new immutable {@link Cache2} instance.
191       */
192      public Cache2<K1,K2,V> build() {
193         return new Cache2<>(this);
194      }
195
196      /**
197       * Sets the caching mode for this cache.
198       *
199       * <p>
200       * Available modes:
201       * <ul>
202       *    <li>{@link CacheMode#NONE NONE} - No caching (always invoke supplier)
203       *    <li>{@link CacheMode#WEAK WEAK} - Weak caching (uses {@link WeakHashMap})
204       *    <li>{@link CacheMode#FULL FULL} - Full caching (uses {@link ConcurrentHashMap}, default)
205       * </ul>
206       *
207       * @param value The caching mode.
208       * @return This object for method chaining.
209       */
210      public Builder<K1,K2,V> cacheMode(CacheMode value) {
211         cacheMode = value;
212         return this;
213      }
214
215      /**
216       * Conditionally enables logging of cache statistics when the JVM exits.
217       *
218       * <p>
219       * When enabled, the cache will register a shutdown hook that logs the cache name,
220       * total cache hits, and total cache misses (size of cache) to help analyze cache effectiveness.
221       *
222       * @param value Whether to enable logging on exit.
223       * @param idValue The identifier to use in the log message.
224       * @return This object for method chaining.
225       */
226      public Builder<K1,K2,V> logOnExit(boolean value, String idValue) {
227         id = idValue;
228         logOnExit = value;
229         return this;
230      }
231
232      /**
233       * Enables logging of cache statistics when the JVM exits.
234       *
235       * <p>
236       * When enabled, the cache will register a shutdown hook that logs the cache name,
237       * total cache hits, and total cache misses (size of cache) to help analyze cache effectiveness.
238       *
239       * <p>
240       * Example output:
241       * <p class='bconsole'>
242       *    ClassMeta cache:  hits=1523, misses: 47
243       * </p>
244       *
245       * <p>
246       * This is useful for:
247       * <ul>
248       *    <li>Performance tuning and identifying caching opportunities
249       *    <li>Determining optimal max size values
250       *    <li>Monitoring cache efficiency in production
251       * </ul>
252       *
253       * @param value The identifier to use in the log message.
254       * @return This object for method chaining.
255       */
256      public Builder<K1,K2,V> logOnExit(String value) {
257         id = value;
258         logOnExit = true;
259         return this;
260      }
261
262      /**
263       * Specifies the maximum number of entries allowed in this cache.
264       *
265       * <p>
266       * When the cache size exceeds this value, the <em>entire</em> cache is cleared to make room for new entries.
267       * This is a simple eviction strategy that avoids the overhead of LRU/LFU tracking.
268       *
269       * <p>
270       * Default value: 1000 (or value of system property <c>juneau.cache.maxSize</c>)
271       *
272       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5>
273       * <ul>
274       *    <li>Setting this too low may cause excessive cache clearing and reduce effectiveness
275       *    <li>Setting this too high may consume excessive memory
276       *    <li>For unbounded caching, use {@link Integer#MAX_VALUE} (not recommended for production)
277       * </ul>
278       *
279       * @param value The maximum number of cache entries. Must be positive.
280       * @return This object for method chaining.
281       */
282      public Builder<K1,K2,V> maxSize(int value) {
283         maxSize = value;
284         return this;
285      }
286
287      /**
288       * Specifies the default supplier function for computing values when keys are not found.
289       *
290       * <p>
291       * This supplier will be used by {@link Cache2#get(Object, Object)} when a key pair is not in the cache.
292       * Individual lookups can override this supplier using
293       * {@link Cache2#get(Object, Object, java.util.function.Supplier)}.
294       *
295       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
296       * <p class='bjava'>
297       *    Cache2&lt;String,Integer,User&gt; <jv>cache</jv> = Cache2
298       *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>, User.<jk>class</jk>)
299       *       .supplier((tenant, id) -&gt; userService.findUser(tenant, id))
300       *       .build();
301       *
302       *    <jc>// Uses default supplier</jc>
303       *    User <jv>u</jv> = <jv>cache</jv>.get(<js>"tenant1"</js>, 123);
304       * </p>
305       *
306       * @param value The default supplier function. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
307       * @return This object for method chaining.
308       */
309      public Builder<K1,K2,V> supplier(Function2<K1,K2,V> value) {
310         supplier = value;
311         return this;
312      }
313
314      /**
315       * Enables thread-local caching.
316       *
317       * <p>
318       * When enabled, each thread gets its own separate cache instance. This is useful for
319       * thread-unsafe objects that need to be cached per thread.
320       *
321       * <p>
322       * This is a shortcut for wrapping a cache in a {@link ThreadLocal}, but provides a cleaner API.
323       *
324       * @return This object for method chaining.
325       * @see Cache.Builder#threadLocal()
326       */
327      public Builder<K1,K2,V> threadLocal() {
328         threadLocal = true;
329         return this;
330      }
331
332      /**
333       * Sets the caching mode to {@link CacheMode#WEAK WEAK}.
334       *
335       * <p>
336       * This is a shortcut for calling <c>cacheMode(CacheMode.WEAK)</c>.
337       *
338       * <p>
339       * Weak caching uses {@link WeakHashMap} for storage, allowing cache entries to be
340       * garbage collected when keys are no longer strongly referenced elsewhere.
341       *
342       * @return This object for method chaining.
343       * @see #cacheMode(CacheMode)
344       */
345      public Builder<K1,K2,V> weak() {
346         return cacheMode(WEAK);
347      }
348
349   }
350
351   /**
352    * Creates a new {@link Builder} for constructing a cache with explicit type parameters.
353    *
354    * <p>
355    * This variant allows you to specify the cache's generic types explicitly without passing
356    * the class objects, which is useful when working with complex parameterized types.
357    *
358    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
359    * <p class='bjava'>
360    *    <jc>// Working with complex generic types</jc>
361    *    Cache2&lt;Class&lt;?&gt;,Class&lt;? extends Annotation&gt;,List&lt;Annotation&gt;&gt; <jv>cache</jv> =
362    *       Cache2.&lt;Class&lt;?&gt;,Class&lt;? extends Annotation&gt;,List&lt;Annotation&gt;&gt;<jsm>create</jsm>()
363    *          .supplier((k1, k2) -&gt; findAnnotations(k1, k2))
364    *          .build();
365    * </p>
366    *
367    * @param <K1> The first key type.
368    * @param <K2> The second key type.
369    * @param <V> The value type.
370    * @return A new builder for configuring the cache.
371    */
372   public static <K1,K2,V> Builder<K1,K2,V> create() {
373      return new Builder<>();
374   }
375
376   /**
377    * Creates a new {@link Builder} for constructing a cache.
378    *
379    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
380    * <p class='bjava'>
381    *    Cache2&lt;String,Integer,User&gt; <jv>cache</jv> = Cache2
382    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>, User.<jk>class</jk>)
383    *       .maxSize(100)
384    *       .build();
385    * </p>
386    *
387    * @param <K1> The first key type.
388    * @param <K2> The second key type.
389    * @param <V> The value type.
390    * @param key1 The first key type class (used for type safety).
391    * @param key2 The second key type class (used for type safety).
392    * @param type The value type class.
393    * @return A new builder for configuring the cache.
394    */
395   @SuppressWarnings({
396      "java:S1172" // Parameters required for type inference in public API
397   })
398   public static <K1,K2,V> Builder<K1,K2,V> of(Class<K1> key1, Class<K2> key2, Class<V> type) {
399      return new Builder<>();
400   }
401
402   // Internal map with Tuple2 keys for content-based equality (especially for arrays)
403   // If threadLocal is true, this is null and threadLocalMap is used instead
404   private final java.util.Map<Tuple2<K1,K2>,V> map;
405
406   @SuppressWarnings({
407      "java:S5164" // Cleanup method provided: cleanup()
408   })
409   private final ThreadLocal<Map<Tuple2<K1,K2>,V>> threadLocalMap;
410
411   private final boolean isThreadLocal;
412
413   private final int maxSize;
414   private final CacheMode cacheMode;
415   private final Function2<K1,K2,V> supplier;
416   private final AtomicInteger cacheHits = new AtomicInteger();
417
418   /**
419    * Constructor.
420    *
421    * @param builder The builder containing configuration settings.
422    */
423   protected Cache2(Builder<K1,K2,V> builder) {
424      this.maxSize = builder.maxSize;
425      this.cacheMode = builder.cacheMode;
426      this.supplier = builder.supplier;
427      this.isThreadLocal = builder.threadLocal;
428
429      if (isThreadLocal) {
430         // Thread-local mode: each thread gets its own map
431         if (builder.cacheMode == WEAK) {
432            this.threadLocalMap = ThreadLocal.withInitial(() -> Collections.synchronizedMap(new WeakHashMap<>()));
433         } else {
434            this.threadLocalMap = ThreadLocal.withInitial(() -> new ConcurrentHashMap<>());
435         }
436         this.map = null;
437      } else {
438         // Normal mode: shared map across all threads
439         if (builder.cacheMode == WEAK) {
440            this.map = Collections.synchronizedMap(new WeakHashMap<>());
441         } else {
442            this.map = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
443         }
444         this.threadLocalMap = null;
445      }
446      if (builder.logOnExit) {
447         shutdownMessage(() -> builder.id + ":  hits=" + cacheHits.get() + ", misses: " + size());
448      }
449   }
450
451   /**
452    * Removes all entries from the cache.
453    */
454   public void clear() {
455      getMap().clear();
456   }
457
458   /**
459    * Cleans up thread-local storage for the current thread.
460    *
461    * <p>
462    * This method should be called when a thread is finished using this cache to prevent memory leaks
463    * in thread pool environments where threads are reused.
464    * Only has an effect if this cache was created with thread-local mode enabled.
465    */
466   public void cleanup() {
467      if (isThreadLocal && threadLocalMap != null) {
468         threadLocalMap.remove();
469      }
470   }
471
472   /**
473    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the cache contains a mapping for the specified key pair.
474    *
475    * @param key1 The first key. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
476    * @param key2 The second key. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
477    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the cache contains the key pair.
478    */
479   public boolean containsKey(K1 key1, K2 key2) {
480      return getMap().containsKey(Tuple2.of(key1, key2));
481   }
482
483   /**
484    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the cache contains one or more entries with the specified value.
485    *
486    * @param value The value to check.
487    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the cache contains the value.
488    */
489   public boolean containsValue(V value) {
490      // ConcurrentHashMap doesn't allow null values, so null can never be in the cache
491      if (value == null)
492         return false;
493      return getMap().containsValue(value);
494   }
495
496   /**
497    * Retrieves a cached value by key pair using the default supplier.
498    *
499    * <p>
500    * This method uses the default supplier configured via {@link Builder#supplier(Function2)}.
501    * If no default supplier was configured, this method will throw a {@link NullPointerException}.
502    *
503    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
504    * <p class='bjava'>
505    *    Cache2&lt;String,Integer,User&gt; <jv>cache</jv> = Cache2
506    *       .<jsm>of</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>, User.<jk>class</jk>)
507    *       .supplier((tenant, id) -&gt; userService.findUser(tenant, id))
508    *       .build();
509    *
510    *    <jc>// Uses default supplier</jc>
511    *    User <jv>u</jv> = <jv>cache</jv>.get(<js>"tenant1"</js>, 123);
512    * </p>
513    *
514    * @param key1 First key component. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
515    * @param key2 Second key component. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
516    * @return The cached or computed value. May be <jk>null</jk> if the supplier returns <jk>null</jk>.
517    * @throws NullPointerException if no default supplier was configured.
518    */
519   public V get(K1 key1, K2 key2) {
520      return get(key1, key2, () -> supplier.apply(key1, key2));
521   }
522
523   /**
524    * Retrieves a cached value by key pair, computing it if necessary using the provided supplier.
525    *
526    * <p>
527    * This method implements the cache-aside pattern:
528    * <ol>
529    *    <li>If the key pair exists in the cache, return the cached value (cache hit)
530    *    <li>If the key pair doesn't exist, invoke the supplier to compute the value
531    *    <li>Store the computed value in the cache using
532    *       {@link java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap#putIfAbsent(Object, Object)}
533    *    <li>Return the value
534    * </ol>
535    *
536    * <h5 class='section'>Behavior:</h5>
537    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
538    *    <li>If the cache is disabled, always invokes the supplier without caching
539    *    <li>If the cache exceeds {@link Builder#maxSize(int)}, clears all entries before storing the new value
540    *    <li>Thread-safe: Multiple threads can safely call this method concurrently
541    *    <li>The supplier may be called multiple times for the same key pair in concurrent scenarios
542    *       (due to {@link java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap#putIfAbsent(Object, Object)} semantics)
543    * </ul>
544    *
545    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
546    * <p class='bjava'>
547    *    Cache2&lt;String,Integer,User&gt; <jv>cache</jv> = Cache2.<jsm>of</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>, User.<jk>class</jk>).build();
548    *
549    *    <jc>// First call: fetches user and caches it</jc>
550    *    User <jv>u1</jv> = <jv>cache</jv>.get(<js>"tenant1"</js>, 123, () -&gt; userService.findUser(<js>"tenant1"</js>, 123));
551    *
552    *    <jc>// Second call: returns cached user instantly</jc>
553    *    User <jv>u2</jv> = <jv>cache</jv>.get(<js>"tenant1"</js>, 123, () -&gt; userService.findUser(<js>"tenant1"</js>, 123));
554    *
555    *    <jsm>assert</jsm> <jv>u1</jv> == <jv>u2</jv>;  <jc>// Same instance</jc>
556    * </p>
557    *
558    * @param key1 First key component. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
559    * @param key2 Second key component. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
560    * @param supplier The supplier to compute the value if it's not in the cache. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
561    * @return The cached or computed value. May be <jk>null</jk> if the supplier returns <jk>null</jk>.
562    */
563   public V get(K1 key1, K2 key2, java.util.function.Supplier<V> supplier) {
564      assertArgNotNull(ARG_supplier, supplier);
565      if (cacheMode == NONE)
566         return supplier.get();
567      var m = getMap();
568      var wrapped = Tuple2.of(key1, key2);
569      V v = m.get(wrapped);
570      if (v == null) {
571         if (size() > maxSize)
572            clear();
573         v = supplier.get();
574         if (v == null)
575            m.remove(wrapped);
576         else
577            m.putIfAbsent(wrapped, v);
578      } else {
579         cacheHits.incrementAndGet();
580      }
581      return v;
582   }
583
584   /**
585    * Returns the total number of cache hits since this cache was created.
586    *
587    * <p>
588    * A cache hit occurs when {@link #get(Object, Object)} or
589    * {@link #get(Object, Object, java.util.function.Supplier)} finds an existing cached value
590    * for the requested key pair, avoiding the need to invoke the supplier.
591    *
592    * <h5 class='section'>Cache Effectiveness:</h5>
593    * <p>
594    * You can calculate the cache hit ratio using:
595    * <p class='bjava'>
596    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>hits</jv> = <jv>cache</jv>.getCacheHits();
597    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>misses</jv> = <jv>cache</jv>.size();
598    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>total</jv> = <jv>hits</jv> + <jv>misses</jv>;
599    *    <jk>double</jk> <jv>hitRatio</jv> = (<jk>double</jk>) <jv>hits</jv> / <jv>total</jv>;  <jc>// 0.0 to 1.0</jc>
600    * </p>
601    *
602    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5>
603    * <ul>
604    *    <li>This counter is never reset, even when {@link #clear()} is called
605    *    <li>Thread-safe using {@link AtomicInteger}
606    *    <li>Returns 0 if the cache is disabled
607    * </ul>
608    *
609    * @return The total number of cache hits since creation.
610    */
611   public int getCacheHits() { return cacheHits.get(); }
612
613   /**
614    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the cache contains no entries.
615    *
616    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the cache is empty.
617    */
618   public boolean isEmpty() { return getMap().isEmpty(); }
619
620   /**
621    * Associates the specified value with the specified key pair.
622    *
623    * @param key1 The first key. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
624    * @param key2 The second key. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
625    * @param value The value to associate with the key pair.
626    * @return The previous value associated with the key pair, or <jk>null</jk> if there was no mapping.
627    */
628   public V put(K1 key1, K2 key2, V value) {
629      var m = getMap();
630      if (value == null)
631         return m.remove(Tuple2.of(key1, key2));
632      return m.put(Tuple2.of(key1, key2), value);
633   }
634
635   /**
636    * Removes the entry for the specified key pair from the cache.
637    *
638    * @param key1 The first key. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
639    * @param key2 The second key. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
640    * @return The previous value associated with the key pair, or <jk>null</jk> if there was no mapping.
641    */
642   public V remove(K1 key1, K2 key2) {
643      return getMap().remove(Tuple2.of(key1, key2));
644   }
645
646   /**
647    * Returns the number of entries in the cache.
648    *
649    * @return The number of cached entries.
650    */
651   public int size() {
652      return getMap().size();
653   }
654
655   /**
656    * Gets the map for the current thread.
657    *
658    * @return The map for the current thread.
659    */
660   private Map<Tuple2<K1,K2>,V> getMap() { return isThreadLocal ? threadLocalMap.get() : map; }
661}
